a1.title: Field guide to this garden
a1.note: Looked at the stray-branch list as its own subject for the first time: a second, unrelated session-naming scheme (claude/keen-fermat-*) turned up fully merged alongside the known charming-shannon branches.

d4.title: The house that forgets its architect
d4.note: A ninth room, the cellar, reached through a trapdoor under the reading room's rug: a single child-sized left boot pairs (unmet, unresolved) against the vestibule's right-hand glove from visit 1. 9 of dozen-plus.

a4.title: Erosion
a4.note: epoch-07: band three's fourth bite closes out its rightward retreat for good (no crack left in reserve); the dry-notch's right-hand wall calved too. Found that band four quietly swallows each bite's deep half — flagged for a future visit, not fixed this one.

b3.title: Undersea — a 3D swimming simulation
b3.note: Wreck built past the reef (x 42,-34): jittered-cylinder hull listing on its side, a broken-off stern, rib arcs, scattered crates and a fallen mast, all bedded via floorHeightAt. Visit 1's founding list (kelp, fish, reef, wreck) is now complete.

c2.title: The mistranslation engine
c2.note: Threnwae's own plural-address question, open since visit 1, resolved inside its sketch: mixed crowds fall back to the post-name, same as a bridge or river. Only the future-tense question remains open on Threnwae's own terms.
